And if you've done everything right, and you've really nailed your pre-launch, there's gonna be some people like that, but not everyone's gonna be like that. ## [03:03 - 06:06]

What's it costing them in three months? What's it costing them in a year, in five years and 30 years? And in the headline, you're typically describing the big benefit, how is their life gonna change? Then you wanna subhead in general to describe the problem and the main benefit of the solution. ## [06:06 - 09:08]

So you might not have the classical headline like you would see in a sales letter, but what is that first big promise that you're gonna make in your sales video? Authority is what makes people believe you. Then you wanna think about proof and testimonials. And then, you know, we're getting, and this is gonna take up the bulk of, or at least the first 50% of your sales video or your sales letter or your sales script. Then you wanna foreshadow urgency. a superior solution to anything out there. Then there's, how does it work? This is some details. ## [12:08 - 15:08]

You do want, this is a place where you have to cover in detail what are the payment methods, what is, if there's a payment plan, what that payment plan is, how often the payments are due and for how long and how much they are. And then you tell them what to do. Click the button, fill out the form, and then this will happen. Now in here, you want to, you know, then this is where you deliver on the scarcity, on the urgency. And, you know, there can be all kinds of different ways to do this. You could have a quantity limit, like on how many are available, physical limit on how many are available. Like if you've got a seminar, it's just natural, that seminar is going to come and go, and there's probably going to be a limited number of seats. If it's a physical product, maybe you only have a certain number available. It could be a workload limit, you can only work with so many people at a time. You could do fast action bonuses, the first X number of customers get this extra bonus. Then, you know, what we use in product launch formula is a schedule limit. It's like, we have, you know, we run a class. So it's time, class is about to get started, so get enrolled, you know, class is starting on Monday. And then, you know, you could just have a deadline that we are pulling the product off the market. So in general, when you're talking about urgency, there's three big ones that it sort of falls into. Is that either the product is coming off the market, the price is going up, or bonuses are going away. Those are the three real big ones. And again, product is coming off the market, price is going up, bonuses are going away. And I'll tell you what, the most important, or not the most important, the most effective of those is the product is coming off the market. But you might not want to pull your product off the market, but the thing is, is at the end of your open cart period, there's something has to happen, so the deal is not as good after that open cart period. Absolutely critical. So then, after you go through the urgency, whatever it is, and it could be a combination of any of these, anything I mentioned, it does have to be just one. Then you have your call to action, which is you ask for the sale. You ask them to go ahead and click the buy button. And I do have here, you can test the different text for the actual order button. And that can definitely impact your conversion. So that's a good thing to test. Then at this point, I like to go with risk reversal and then a surprise bonus. So what I'll do is the call to action, then I'll do the risk reversal, which is a guarantee. And then come in with a surprise bonus,

## [15:08 - 15:56]

and then a reclose. And you don't have to do a surprise bonus. I don't always do that, but you absolutely have to do the call to action, the risk reversal, and then a reclose. And this is where you do, you revisit that scarcity and why they have to act right now. If you want to, after that, you can come in with some more social proof, which would be testimonials, blog comments, the number of people you've bought, the results they've gotten, anything like that. But then if you do that, you do want to come in always, at the end, a final call to action, a final call to order. That should always be the last thing out of your mouth when you go to close your video, close your sales letter.
